info.title: Together API
info.description: Private, non-federated Discord alternative for small gaming communities

get.summary: GET /admin/servers — Paginated server list with search and enriched counts.
get.description:
Query parameters:
- `page` (default 1)
- `per_page` (default 50, max 100)
- `search` — ILIKE match on server name

delete.description:
Deletes the server row; foreign key cascades handle channels, messages,
memberships, etc.

get.summary: GET /admin/settings — Get instance-wide settings (admin only).
GET /admin/stats — Instance overview statistics.
get.description:
Returns aggregate counts, active WebSocket connections, uptime, DB latency,
and upload directory storage size.

get.summary: GET /admin/users — Paginated user list with search and sorting.
get.description:
Query parameters:
- `page` (default 1)
- `per_page` (default 50, max 100)
- `search` — ILIKE match on username or email
- `sort_by` — one of: `username`, `created_at`, `message_count` (default `created_at`)

delete.description:
Transaction: delete sessions, anonymize messages (SET author_id = NULL),
then delete the user row (cascades memberships, DM participants, etc.).
Self-guard: cannot delete yourself.

patch.description:
Self-operation guards: cannot demote or disable yourself (prevents admin lockout).
On disable: sets `disabled = true`, `disabled_at = NOW()`, deletes all sessions.
On enable: sets `disabled = false`, `disabled_at = NULL`.

post.summary: POST /auth/forgot-password — Generate a password reset token for a user.
post.description:
Admin-only endpoint. Returns the reset token in the response body for
manual delivery (e.g., admin sharing with user out-of-band).

post.summary: POST /auth/reset-password — Reset password using token.
post.description:
Validates the reset token and updates the user's password.
Token is single-use and expires after 1 hour.

200.description: List of bots owned by the user

post.summary: POST /bots/connect — Exchange a static bot token for a short-lived JWT.
post.description:
Bots should use this endpoint to obtain a short-lived access token (15 min)
for WebSocket connections via `?token=<jwt>`. This avoids exposing the
static bot token in server/proxy access logs.

Returns: {"access_token": "<jwt>"}

403.description: Only bots can use this endpoint

404.description: Bot not found or already revoked

patch.summary: PATCH /bots/:id — Update bot name and/or description.

get.summary: GET /bots/:id/logs — Retrieve recent activity for a bot.
get.description:
Synthesises a log from existing data: creation, messages sent, and
revocation (if applicable).

post.summary: POST /channels/:channel_id/ack — mark a server channel as read.
post.description:
Upserts the user's read-state to `NOW()`.  Called by the client whenever
the user views a channel (tab focus, navigation).

get.summary: GET /channels/:channel_id/go-live — get the active Go Live session, if any.
get.description: Returns 200 with the session JSON when live, 404 when no session is active.

post.summary: POST /channels/:channel_id/go-live — start a Go Live broadcast.
post.description:
Enforces: one broadcaster per channel, broadcaster must be in the channel.
Broadcasts `GO_LIVE_START` to all server members on success.

delete.summary: DELETE /channels/:channel_id/go-live — end the active Go Live broadcast.
delete.description:
Only the current broadcaster (or a server admin) may stop the session.
Broadcasts `GO_LIVE_STOP` to all server members.

get.summary: GET /channels/:channel_id/messages — list messages with cursor pagination (members only).
get.description:
Returns up to `limit` messages (default 50, max 100), ordered newest-first.
Pass `before=<message_id>` to paginate backwards.

The cursor uses a compound `(created_at, id)` comparison to give a stable
total order even when messages share an identical timestamp.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description:
Cursor: return messages created strictly before the message with this ID.

The ID is resolved to a `(created_at, id)` pair server-side, so the
actual comparison is on timestamp + UUID — not the ID alone. This gives
a stable total order even when two messages share an identical timestamp.

If the cursor ID does not exist or belongs to a different channel the
query returns an empty array (no error).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of messages to return (default 50, max 100).

post.summary: POST /channels/:channel_id/messages — send a message (members only).

get.summary: GET /channels/:channel_id/messages/:message_id — fetch one message (members only).
get.description:
Returns the full MessageDto including deleted messages (deleted flag is set
to true) so reply-bar previews can show "original message deleted" state.

post.summary: POST /channels/:channel_id/messages/:message_id/pin
post.description:
Pin a message in a channel.  Requires the MANAGE_MESSAGES permission
(or ADMINISTRATOR, or server ownership).  Idempotent — pinning an already-
pinned message succeeds without error.

delete.description:
Unpin a message in a channel.  Requires the MANAGE_MESSAGES permission.
Returns 404 if the message is not currently pinned.

get.summary: GET /channels/:channel_id/messages/:message_id/reactions
get.description:
List aggregated reaction counts for a message, with a `me` flag indicating
whether the authenticated user has added each reaction.

put.description:
Add an emoji reaction to a message.  Idempotent — adding the same emoji
twice is not an error (the duplicate is silently ignored).

delete.description:
Remove the authenticated user's reaction from a message.
Returns 404 if the message or reaction does not exist.

get.summary: GET /channels/:channel_id/messages/:message_id/thread — list thread replies.
get.description:
Replies are returned in ascending order (oldest first) — threads read top-to-bottom.
Cursor pagination via `before=<uuid>`: pass the ID of the *newest* reply already
displayed to receive the next page of older replies (used when scrolling up). Replies
that come *after* the cursor in time are not returned; this is appropriate for
history loading. Pass no cursor for the initial load (returns the first page
ordered oldest-first). The `thread_reply_count` field defaults to 0 on these rows
(it is only meaningful on root messages in the channel list).

parameters.name: before
parameters.description:
Cursor: return messages created strictly before the message with this ID.

The ID is resolved to a `(created_at, id)` pair server-side, so the
actual comparison is on timestamp + UUID — not the ID alone. This gives
a stable total order even when two messages share an identical timestamp.

If the cursor ID does not exist or belongs to a different channel the
query returns an empty array (no error).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of messages to return (default 50, max 100).

post.summary: POST /channels/:channel_id/messages/:message_id/thread — post a reply into a thread.
post.description:
The parent message must be a root message (`thread_id IS NULL`). Thread replies
cannot themselves be threaded (no nested threads). Returns 400 if the parent is
already a thread reply.

400.description: Cannot thread off a thread reply

get.summary: GET /channels/:channel_id/overrides — list all permission overrides for a channel.

put.description:
Requires MANAGE_CHANNELS permission. The request must specify exactly one of
`role_id` or `user_id`. The `allow` and `deny` bitfields must not overlap.

get.summary: GET /channels/:channel_id/pinned-messages
get.description:
List all pinned messages in a channel, ordered by pin time (newest first).
Any server member can view pinned messages.

get.summary: GET /channels/:channel_id/voice — list all participants (members only).
get.description:
Each entry includes `username` so the response shape matches the
`VOICE_STATE_UPDATE` WebSocket broadcast events.

200.description: List of voice participants. Each entry extends VoiceStateDto with an additional `username: String` field.

post.summary: POST /channels/:channel_id/voice — join a voice channel.
post.description:
Uses UPSERT to atomically move the user from any prior channel to this one.
`self_mute`, `self_deaf`, `self_video`, and `self_screen` are reset to `false` on channel switch.
`server_mute` and `server_deaf` are intentionally preserved so
moderator-applied restrictions survive channel switches.

If the user was in a voice channel on a *different* server, a
`VOICE_STATE_UPDATE` leave event is broadcast to that server so its
members do not see a ghost participant.

delete.description: Returns 404 if the user is not currently in this specific channel.

patch.description:
At least one field must be provided; an empty body returns 400.
Returns 404 if the user is not currently in this channel.
Only `self_mute` and `self_deaf` are accepted; `server_mute`/`server_deaf`
are excluded from the request type to prevent privilege escalation.

get.summary: GET /dm-channels — list all DM channels for the authenticated user.

post.summary: POST /dm-channels — open or retrieve an existing DM channel with another user.
post.description:
Idempotent: if a channel already exists between the two users, it is
returned rather than creating a duplicate.

Uses a PostgreSQL advisory transaction lock keyed to the sorted pair of
user UUIDs so that concurrent requests cannot create duplicate channels.
The lock is released automatically when the transaction commits or rolls back.

post.summary: POST /dm-channels/:channel_id/ack — mark a DM channel as read.

get.summary: GET /dm-channels/:id/messages — list messages in a DM channel with cursor pagination.

post.summary: POST /dm-channels/:id/messages — send a message to a DM channel.

get.summary: GET /emojis/:emoji_id — serve a custom emoji image (public, no auth required).
get.description:
Responds with the raw image bytes and appropriate `Content-Type`.
Sets `Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400` (24 h) since emoji images are
immutable once uploaded (delete and re-upload creates a new ID).

get.summary: GET /files/:message_id/*filepath — serve an attachment file (members only).
get.description:
Authorization and membership are checked before serving the file.
The attachment URL is verified against the database so that only files
successfully recorded in the DB are accessible.

get.summary: GET /health — Detailed health status for monitoring systems.
get.description:
Returns comprehensive health information including database connectivity
and latency, WebSocket connection count, server uptime, and service version.

Returns `200 OK` when all checks pass, `503 Service Unavailable` if any
check fails.

get.summary: GET /health/live — Liveness check for monitoring and deployment systems.
get.description:
Returns `200 OK` if the process is alive and able to respond to requests.
This is intentionally lightweight and queries no external dependencies —
if this handler can respond, the process is not deadlocked.

get.summary: GET /health/ready — Readiness check for monitoring and deployment systems.
get.description:
Returns `200 OK` if the service is ready to accept traffic.
Returns `503 Service Unavailable` if any critical dependency is unavailable.

Checks:
- Database connectivity

get.summary: GET /ice-servers — returns STUN/TURN server configurations.
get.description:
Always returns public STUN servers. If TURN servers are configured,
generates time-limited credentials using HMAC-SHA1.

Authorization: Requires authentication (user must be logged in).

get.summary: GET /instance/registration-mode — Public endpoint (no auth required).
get.description:
Returns the current registration mode so the login page can show/hide
the register button and invite code field.

get.summary: Preview an invite — any authenticated user can view server info before joining.

post.summary: Accept an invite and join the server.

400.description: Invite expired or max uses reached

get.summary: GET /link-preview?url=<encoded-url>
get.description:
Returns Open Graph metadata for the given URL, with results cached for 24 hours.
Requires authentication. Rejects private/loopback IPs (SSRF protection).

delete.summary: DELETE /messages/:message_id — soft-delete a message (author or server owner).
delete.description: The message row is retained with `deleted = TRUE`; no content is returned.

403.description: Not the message author or server owner

patch.summary: PATCH /messages/:message_id — edit a message's content (author only).

get.summary: GET /messages/:message_id/attachments — list attachments for a message (members only).

post.summary: POST /messages/:message_id/attachments — upload one or more files (author only).
post.description:
Expects a `multipart/form-data` body with one or more file fields named `files`.
Each file is written to `{upload_dir}/{message_id}/{uuid}_{filename}` on disk
and returned with a URL of `/files/{message_id}/{uuid}_{filename}`.

Authorization rules:
- Caller must be authenticated.
- Caller must be a member of the server that owns the channel.
- Caller must be the message author.

Validation:
- Each file must be non-empty and ≤ 50 MB.
- The combined attachment count for the message cannot exceed 10.

The upload is atomic: all validation happens before any file is written to disk.
If a disk write or database insert fails, any files already written are removed
and the database transaction is rolled back.

get.summary: GET /server-templates — list all available server templates.
GET /servers — list all servers the authenticated user belongs to.

post.summary: POST /servers — create a new server; creator is auto-joined as owner.

get.summary: GET /servers/browse — list all public servers (authenticated, no membership required).
get.description:
Returns servers ordered by member count (descending) then creation date.
Results are capped at 50 — discovery is intentionally lightweight with no pagination.
Does NOT filter out servers the caller already belongs to; clients derive "Joined"
state by cross-referencing their own server list.

get.summary: GET /servers/:id — get a single server (members only).

delete.summary: DELETE /servers/:id — delete server and all its data (owner only).

patch.summary: PATCH /servers/:id — update name, icon, or public visibility (owner only).

get.summary: GET /servers/:id/audit-logs — List audit logs for a server.
get.description:
Only the server owner can view audit logs.
Supports filtering by action, actor, and target type.
Paginated with cursor-based pagination.

get.summary: GET /servers/:id/automod — Returns 404 if no config exists yet.
get.description: Only the server owner can view automod settings.

patch.summary: PATCH /servers/:id/automod — Upsert automod config with partial updates via COALESCE.
patch.description: Only the server owner can update automod settings.

get.summary: GET /servers/:id/automod/logs — List the 100 most recent automod actions.
get.description: Only the server owner can view automod logs.

get.summary: GET /servers/:id/automod/words — List all word filters for a server.
get.description: Only the server owner can view word filters.

post.summary: POST /servers/:id/automod/words — Add a word to the filter list.
post.description:
Only the server owner can add word filters.
Words are normalized to lowercase before storage.

delete.summary: DELETE /servers/:id/automod/words/:word — Remove a word from the filter list.
delete.description:
Only the server owner can remove word filters.
Silently succeeds even if the word was not in the list.

get.summary: GET /servers/:id/bans — List all bans for a server.
get.description: Requires BAN_MEMBERS permission (or server owner / ADMINISTRATOR).

delete.summary: DELETE /servers/:id/bans/:user_id — Unban a user from a server.
delete.description:
Requires BAN_MEMBERS permission (or server owner / ADMINISTRATOR).
Silently succeeds even if the user was not banned.

get.summary: GET /servers/:id/channels — list all channels in a server (members only).

post.summary: POST /servers/:id/channels — create a channel in a server (owner only).

get.summary: GET /servers/:id/channels/:channel_id — get a single channel (members only).

delete.description:
This is a hard delete; any messages in the channel are also removed by the
database cascade constraint (ON DELETE CASCADE on messages.channel_id).

get.summary: GET /servers/:server_id/emojis — list all custom emojis for a server.
get.description: Authorization: caller must be a member of the server.

post.summary: POST /servers/:server_id/emojis — upload a custom emoji image.
post.description:
Authorization: caller must have the MANAGE_EMOJIS permission (or be the owner).

Expects a `multipart/form-data` body with:
- `name`  — text field: emoji name (1–32 chars, `[a-z0-9_-]` only)
- `image` — file field: image bytes (JPEG / PNG / GIF / WebP, ≤ 256 KB)

On success returns `201 Created` with the created emoji DTO.

delete.description:
Authorization: caller must have the MANAGE_EMOJIS permission (or be the owner).
Returns `204 No Content` on success.

get.summary: GET /servers/:id/export — owner-only.
get.description:
Builds a ZIP archive in memory from live DB queries and streams it back as
an `application/zip` download.  Nothing is written to disk.

ZIP layout:
  {server-slug}-export/
    server.json                          — server metadata
    channels.json                        — all channels
    members.json                         — member list (no credentials)
    roles.json                           — role definitions
    messages/{channel-slug}-{id}.jsonl   — newline-delimited JSON per text channel
    dm_messages/{user-slug}-{id}.jsonl   — requesting user's DMs

post.summary: POST /servers/:id/join — join a public server as the authenticated user.
post.description:
Private servers (`is_public = false`) return 404 to avoid leaking their
existence. Membership-by-invite is not yet implemented.

get.summary: GET /servers/:id/members — list all members of a server (members only).

post.summary: POST /servers/:id/members/:user_id/ban
post.description: Ban a member from the server (remove + prevent rejoin). Requires BAN_MEMBERS permission.

post.summary: POST /servers/:id/members/:user_id/kick
post.description: Remove a member from the server. Requires KICK_MEMBERS permission.

put.summary: PUT /servers/:id/members/:user_id/roles/:role_id — assign a role to a member.

delete.summary: DELETE /servers/:id/members/:user_id/roles/:role_id — remove a role from a member.

post.summary: POST /servers/:id/members/:user_id/timeout
post.description: Timeout a member for N minutes. Requires MUTE_MEMBERS permission.

delete.description: Remove an active timeout from a member. Requires MUTE_MEMBERS permission.

get.summary: GET /servers/:id/roles — list all roles in a server.

post.summary: POST /servers/:id/roles — create a new role.

get.summary: GET /servers/:id/search — search messages in a server.
get.description:
Full-text search using PostgreSQL `to_tsvector` and `plainto_tsquery`.
Results are ranked by relevance and highlighted with `<mark>` tags.

Authorization: User must be a member of the server.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: Optional channel ID to limit search scope.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination: return results before this message ID.

post.summary: Send a test ping event to the webhook URL.

get.summary:
GET /users/:user_id — fetch any user's public profile.
Returns only public fields; never exposes email or password_hash.

AdminListQuery.description: Query parameters for admin paginated list endpoints.

Attachment.description: A file attached to a message.

AuditLog.description: Audit log entry for admin actions.

BotCreatedResponse.description:
Response for POST /bots and POST /bots/:id/token/regenerate.
Token is shown exactly once and never stored in plaintext.

token.description: Plaintext token — shown once at creation/regeneration only.

BotDto.description:
Public bot shape returned by REST API responses.
token_hash is intentionally excluded.

BotLogEntry.description: A single entry in a bot's activity log.

ChannelPermissionOverride.description: Per-channel permission override for a role or user.

CheckResult.description: Result of a single readiness check.

error.description: Human-readable failure category, present only when `ok` is false.

options.description: 2 to 10 option texts; IDs are generated server-side

icon_url.description: Must be a valid HTTP(S) URL when provided.

CreateThreadReplyRequest.description:
Request body for posting a reply into a thread.

Intentionally excludes `reply_to` — thread replies are always children of
the root message identified by the URL parameter, never quote-replies.

CustomEmojiDto.description: API response shape for a custom emoji (omits internal `filename`).

latency_ms.description:
Present on both success and failure. On failure this reflects the time
elapsed before the error (e.g. a timeout shows the full timeout duration).

DirectMessage.description:
A message sent inside a DM channel.

`author_id` is `None` when the originating user account has been deleted
(the foreign key has `ON DELETE SET NULL`). Clients should render deleted
accounts as "Deleted User".

DirectMessageChannel.description: A private channel shared between exactly two users.

DirectMessageChannelDto.description: A DM channel enriched with participant info for API responses.

last_message_at.description:
Timestamp of the most recent non-deleted message, used for list
ordering and last-active display. `None` when no messages exist yet.

recipient.description: The other participant (not the requesting user).

GoLiveSession.description:
An active Go Live broadcast session within a voice channel.

At most one session exists per channel at a time — enforced by the
`start_go_live` handler under a write lock.

uptime_secs.description: `null` if uptime tracking was not initialized.

IceServersResponse.description: Response containing ICE servers with time-limited TURN credentials.

ttl.description: Time-to-live for the credentials in seconds.

InvitePreviewDto.description: Preview information shown before accepting an invite.

LinkPreviewDto.description:
Open Graph metadata returned by `GET /link-preview`.

All fields except `url` are optional — a page may have no OG tags.

ListAuditLogsQuery.description: Query parameters for listing audit logs.

MemberDto.description: Member of a server, combining user fields with membership metadata.

MemberRoleInfo.description: Lightweight role info attached to member responses (no server_id/permissions).

MemberWithRolesDto.description: Member response enriched with role information.

pinned.description:
Whether this message has been pinned in its channel.
Defaults to false; not included in some validation-only queries.

pinned_at.description: When this message was pinned, if pinned.

pinned_by.description: The user who pinned this message, if pinned.

thread_id.description: Set on thread replies; `None` on root messages.

thread_reply_count.description:
Number of non-deleted thread replies on a root message.
Populated only by `list_messages` via a subquery; defaults to 0
on insert RETURNING and thread-reply list queries.

MessageDto.description: API response for a message. Wraps Message with optional rich content.

oneOf.description: Some when the message was created by /event
Some when the message was created by /poll

user_id.description: The ID of the user to open a DM with.

user_vote.description: The option_id the calling user voted for, or None

PublicProfileDto.description: Public profile shape for GET /users/:id — omits private fields like email.

ReactionCount.description: Aggregated reaction count for a single emoji on a message.

me.description:
Whether the requesting user has added this reaction at the time of the
query. Clients must apply `REACTION_ADD`/`REACTION_REMOVE` WebSocket
delta events to keep this accurate after the initial fetch.

invite_code.description: Required when instance registration mode is "invite_only".

password.description: max = 128 prevents bcrypt's 72-byte truncation from becoming a DoS vector

Role.description: A role within a server, carrying permission bitflags and display metadata.

before.description: Cursor for pagination: return results before this message ID.

channel_id.description: Optional channel ID to limit search scope.

total.description: Total matching messages (approximate for large result sets).

SearchResult.description: A single search result with highlighted snippet.

highlight.description: HTML snippet with matching terms wrapped in <mark> tags.

ServerDto.description: Server enriched with live member count for API responses.

ServerInvite.description: Database row for a server invite link.

ServerTemplateDto.description: Public API shape for a server template.

TemplateChannelDto.description: A single channel definition inside a template's JSONB data.

UnreadCount.description:
Unread summary returned in the READY event.

`unread_count` is always >= 1 (the query uses `HAVING COUNT > 0`).
Channels with no `channel_read_states` row are omitted entirely — this
means "never acknowledged" and "zero unread" are indistinguishable until
the user first acknowledges the channel.

icon_url.description: Must be a valid HTTP(S) URL when provided.

activity.description: Activity/rich presence text, e.g. "Playing Minecraft"; capped at 128 characters.

avatar_url.description: Must be a valid HTTP(S) URL when provided.

custom_status.description: Free-form status text; capped at 128 characters.

pronouns.description: Pronouns string, e.g. "they/them"; capped at 40 characters.

UpdateVoiceStateRequest.description:
Request body for PATCH /channels/:id/voice.

Only user-controlled flags are accepted; `server_mute`/`server_deaf` are
excluded at the type level to prevent privilege escalation. Unknown fields
are rejected (deny_unknown_fields) rather than silently ignored.

UserDto.description: Public user shape returned by all API responses.

VoiceStateDto.description:
Wire representation of voice state for REST responses and broadcast events.

`channel_id` and `joined_at` are `None` when representing a user who has
left all voice channels (used in `VOICE_STATE_UPDATE` leave broadcasts).
This is a separate type from `VoiceState` to decouple the API shape from
the DB row and prevent future field additions from accidentally leaking.

WebhookCreatedResponse.description: Response for POST /servers/:id/webhooks — includes the secret (shown once).

secret.description: Plaintext HMAC signing secret — shown once at creation only.

WebhookDto.description:
Public webhook shape returned by REST API responses.
`secret` is intentionally excluded — shown only at creation.
